ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Indonesian martabak manis; 'pancake food product' is the closest defensible FoodOn class covering thick pan-fried sweet pancakes.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers pancakes and similar products; martabak manis is a thick sweet stuffed pancake variant. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for this Indonesian preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet pancake as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 10 pieces (approximately 500 g), which is the full portion as sold on GrabFood; a smaller reference serving of 3 pieces (approximately 150 g) is also recognised.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe (SR Legacy ID 18259) and Pancakes with fruit filling, composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Indonesian martabak manis (banana chocolate). Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pancake entries (plain and fruit-filled variants) combined with HPB Singapore nutrient data for sweetened condensed milk and chocolate toppings. The input record's per_100g values (energy 300 kcal, protein 5.33 g, fat 13.33 g, carbohydrate 40.0 g, sugar 23.33 g, sodium 166.67 mg, dietary fibre 2.0 g, calcium 53.33 mg, iron 1.0 mg, cholesterol 46.67 mg, saturated fat 6.67 g, trans fat 0.33 g, vitamin C 3.33 mg) are consistent with a composite estimate for this preparation and are retained with medium confidence. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and full micronutrient profiles for this composite preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore database as of 2026.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pancakes, buckwheat and plain wheat analogues; University of Sydney GI Database entry for pancakes (GI ~67).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Indonesian martabak manis (banana chocolate). GI of 67 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain wheat-flour pancakes (GI ~67, medium category). The addition of banana (GI ~51), sugar, and condensed milk increases glycaemic load substantially but does not materially change the GI category from medium. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 67 × carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (67 × 200 g carbohydrate in 500 g serving at 40 g/100 g) / 100 = 134.0. This is an extremely high glycaemic load for a single serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (23.33 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g) triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation, raising blood pressure independently of sodium intake; sodium is 166.67 mg/100 g.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this food: sugar at 23.33 g/100 g far exceeds the AHA threshold of 10 g/100 g for solid foods with fibre below 2 g/100 g, and the saturated fat content (6.67 g/100 g) contributes to arterial stiffness; a plain banana without added sugar and condensed milk would be a more suitable alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 23.33 g/100 g exceeds the 20 g/100 g hard glycaemic gate; carbohydrate 40.0 g/100 g with only 2.0 g/100 g dietary fibre and a GI of ~67 produces a very high glycaemic load of ~134 per 500 g serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This food is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: the combination of high sugar (23.33 g/100 g), high refined carbohydrate (40.0 g/100 g), low fibre (2.0 g/100 g), and sweetened condensed milk creates an extremely high glycaemic load that will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; consumption should be avoided or strictly limited to a single piece (approximately 50 g) on rare occasions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 6.67 g/100 g (from butter and condensed milk) and trans fat 0.33 g/100 g (from margarine) raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis; cholesterol is 46.67 mg/100 g and sugar 23.33 g/100 g drives de novo lipogenesis.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food: saturated fat at 6.67 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g cardiovascular threshold, trans fat at 0.33 g/100 g is present and directly atherogenic, and the high sugar load (23.33 g/100 g) promotes triglyceride synthesis and endothelial inflammation; substituting butter with extra-virgin olive oil and eliminating condensed milk and chocolate sprinkles would substantially improve the cardiovascular risk profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 6.67 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; refined sugar 23.33 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); trans fat 0.33 g/100 g is a hard RA disqualifier; pan-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs).
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This food is not suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis: saturated fat (6.67 g/100 g), trans fat (0.33 g/100 g), and high refined sugar (23.33 g/100 g) collectively promote systemic inflammation; replacing butter/margarine with extra-virgin olive oil and eliminating condensed milk and chocolate sprinkles would reduce the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Banana Chocolate Martabak (Martabak Manis Pisang Coklat) is a thick, fluffy Indonesian sweet pancake filled with fresh ripe banana slices and chocolate sprinkles, then drizzled generously with sweetened condensed milk. It is prepared by pan-frying a leavened wheat-flour batter (made with egg, sugar, butter, baking powder, and yeast) in a heavy round mould until the base is golden and the interior is soft and spongy. The filling is added while the pancake is still hot, and the pancake is folded in half and sliced into pieces before serving. The texture is pillowy and moist, with a slightly crisp bottom crust, a sweet banana flavour, and a rich chocolatey finish from the condensed milk drizzle. Martabak manis is a beloved Indonesian street food and night-market staple, widely sold across Indonesia and in Indonesian food stalls in Singapore. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 300 kcal, 5.33 g protein, 13.33 g fat (6.67 g saturated, 0.33 g trans), 40.0 g carbohydrate, 23.33 g sugar, 2.0 g dietary fibre, and 166.67 mg sodium. The high sugar content (23.33 g/100 g), high saturated fat (6.67 g/100 g), and presence of trans fat make this food clinically unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. A 10-piece serving (approximately 500 g) delivers an estimated glycaemic load of approximately 134, which is extremely high. The food is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten, egg, and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in banana choco martabak?
What is the sugar content of martabak manis?
Is martabak manis suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Indonesian martabak manis (banana chocolate) exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pancake entries and composite estimation consistent with the described ingredients. The input record's values are plausible and internally consistent; medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation basis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, full B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this composite Indonesian martabak manis preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore database as of 2026. All core macros (protein_g 5.33, fat_g 13.33, carbohydrate_g 40.0) are non-zero and do not require zero-macro justification. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain wheat-flour pancakes; no direct GI measurement for martabak manis is available in approved sources. Glycaemic load per 500 g serving = (67 × 200) / 100 = 134.0. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) is not an approved primary nutrient source; nutrient values have been validated against USDA FoodData Central composite estimates and are retained at medium confidence. Two portions are listed in the input: 10 pieces (~500 g, default) and 3 pieces (~150 g); the 500 g serving is used as the primary serving size. The food is classified as Indonesian cuisine sold in Singapore; mandatory Indonesian local name is satisfied by 'Martabak Manis Pisang Coklat'. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases have been added to support multilingual retrieval given the Singapore sales context and to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements as interpreted by the validator. Chinese aliases are also included for retrieval completeness. Malay local name: 'Martabak Manis Pisang Coklat' (shared with Indonesian as the term is used across both languages in Singapore). Tamil local name: 'வாழைப்பழம் சாக்லேட் மார்தாபாக்' (transliteration of banana chocolate martabak). Hindi local name: 'केला चॉकलेट मार्ताबाक' (transliteration of banana chocolate martabak).
